description_long: This apartment building complex consists of three distinct, multi-story structures arranged side by side, each with a weathered, dark brick exterior and flat, gray concrete roofs. The central building is the largest, featuring prominent external staircases on both sides and multiple balconies, while the flanking buildings are slightly smaller and simpler in design. The facades are punctuated by rows of rectangular windows, some grouped in pairs or threes, providing a sense of symmetry and rhythm. The ground floors of the buildings include large entryways, with the central building displaying double-arched doors and the leftmost building featuring a red awning above a commercial-style entrance. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, and the surfaces show signs of aging and urban wear. The structures are likely constructed with reinforced concrete and steel framing, typical of mid-20th-century urban apartment blocks.
description: A weathered, multi-story apartment building complex with dark brick exteriors, flat roofs, and prominent external staircases and balconies. The facades feature rows of rectangular windows and utilitarian entryways.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the flat, unadorned backs of the buildings, with minimal window openings and no visible entrances.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the external staircases and balconies on the central building, as well as the blocky, utilitarian profiles of all three structures.
description_view_2: The opposite rear view again shows the plain backs of the buildings, with some balconies and a red awning partially visible.
description_view_3: The front view displays the main facades, including the double-arched entryway, red awning, and the most detailed arrangement of windows and doors.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, symmetrical rows and columns, with some grouped in pairs or threes, providing a consistent and rhythmic pattern across the facades.
